reasoning: I am tired of waiting, but I have not forgotten why I voted in 2019. The paper — the one that says I am Indian — that is what my children will need, not just the rice my hands can hold today. Mamata gives us the rations and I am grateful, but she cannot give me the citizenship. Only the party that wrote CAA can make that real, even if Shantanu Thakur has been sitting there and nothing has happened yet. I vote for the promise that is still ahead, not just for the hand that feeds me today. But I do not vote with hope anymore — I vote because I have no other choice.
primary_drivers: citizenship documentation through CAA — the only promise that addresses my fundamental insecurity
Matua community representation and the belief that only this government will eventually implement the law it wrote
long-term stability for my children over immediate rations
